threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus driver shouts 'next stop!' half a block before you expect it. Trust me, we all used to.
Saw a man serenading the night bus crowd with a ukulele at Clapham South. Almost took the whole ride to work out the chords but got a free show.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was truly a Neuköllner when I jumped on a bus that didn't arrive for 30 minutes, but everyone around me was casually chatting like it was normal.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you hear techno beats blaring from the park while kids play in the sun. It's a wild mix of chaos and calm that feels just right.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their reaction to the BART delays; they shrug it off and just order a burrito instead of panicking.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 38 Geary today - a saxophonist played smooth jazz while the bus swayed. People stopped scrolling for a moment. Pure San Francisco magic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every bar has tapas, but locals know a good vermut is what you really want at 12 on a Sunday.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by the way they stop at every corner to snap photos of doors and colorful tiles—locals just walk past them.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You say 'sorry' at the bar when there's a queue. Locals just elbow their way through like they're catching a bus.
Caught a midnight 25 last week, driver stopped for a full ten minutes while a couple argued about where to go. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I was on the bus when two kids debated if a pigeon was ‘culture’ or just ‘a rat with wings’. That’s when I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you get a spontaneous open-air ‘silent disco’ on a park lawn, complete with disco lights and everyone wearing headphones. It's magical.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know you're local when you can tell which BART delays are BS and which ones are just the usual Friday chaos.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus that pulled over for a street performer’s drum solo. Everyone clapped, then it moved on. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all bars close at 2am, but the locals know many stay open well past dawn, especially on weekends. It's when the real vibe kicks in.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They're often the ones standing at the corner of Carrer de València, looking at the Metro map like it’s an ancient scroll, lost in translation.
